observed_behavior: State-driven matchmaking handler; state 9 logs Request QueryUserInRoomRequest() and dispatches room-user query

observed_behavior: Allocates 0x28-byte room entry, copies payload fields, appends linked-list node, increments room count

observed_behavior: Unlinks room entry by object id and decrements room count

observed_behavior: Room callback sibling copies payload fields at +0x10/+0x14/+0x18 into the active room-object path
server_implication: Live room updates use a distinct room-object callback family in addition to room-user callbacks

observed_behavior: Tiny sibling callback that forwards the room/object id into the active room UI object
server_implication: Some room changes are dispatched as object notifications rather than roster entries

observed_behavior: Updates an existing room object by tag; separate tags change room name, room attributes, and a +0x18 integer consistent with user count

observed_behavior: Finds room by id, updates tagged fields, has explicit room-name and room-attribute/open-locked handling

observed_behavior: Finds lobby by id and updates tagged GAME/NAME/USER fields

observed_behavior: Logs Lobby %d %s Created. on success

observed_behavior: Logs Lobby %d Deleted. on success

observed_behavior: Logs UserID:%d NAME:%s and copies callback payload name data
server_implication: Room user callback payload layout matters directly to client object population

observed_behavior: Active in-room UI handler consumes queued room-user events and separately emits The number of players in this room has changed. when a room-object flag trips
server_implication: Count-change toast and visible roster materialization are related but distinct client paths

observed_behavior: Success wrapper logs Completed ChangeUserStatusCallback. through the current UI/log callback object

observed_behavior: Failure wrapper logs Fail ChangeUserStatusCallback Request. through the same callback object family

observed_behavior: Writes a small state field at row-object offset +0x58 with explicit cases for incoming values 1, 2, and 3

observed_behavior: Copies or clears two separate 0x3c-byte text buffers at row-object offsets +0x9c and +0xd8
server_implication: A blank room-member row is consistent with this setter not receiving the expected non-empty text payload

observed_behavior: Maps lobby query state codes: 0=request, 0x11=complete, 0x12=fail, 0x13=no lobbies
server_implication: Lobby query status handling has explicit per-code behavior

observed_behavior: Maps room query state codes: 0x11=complete, 0x12=fail, 0x13=no rooms, 2/8->state3, 5->state0

observed_behavior: Normalizes callback selector into small result classes
server_implication: Callback routing is class-based, not a single flat handler

observed_behavior: Companion selector helper chooses another table-backed 4-byte record
server_implication: Wrong callback class/record mapping can surface as popup flicker or stalled UI

observed_behavior: Indexes fixed-size 0x20/0x40 records based on normalized selector
server_implication: Client callback records are structured and size-sensitive

observed_behavior: Stores a per-slot flag in the active room UI object and notifies a child presenter when any slot pair is non-zero
server_implication: This is a strong candidate for the dim/bright room-member presentation path

observed_behavior: Forwards a boolean directly to the active room UI presenter and is the concrete target of the final start-enable check in 0x8C096928
server_implication: Server-side ready_to_start is not sufficient if the client-side row/start presenter never reaches this path with a true value

observed_behavior: Pops compact queued event records from a ring-buffer-like structure and feeds 0x8C096928
server_implication: The active room screen is consuming a queued update layer, not just direct room snapshots

observed_behavior: Copies a 10-byte field from payload+0xb, trims it, and pushes a slot-aware row update through the room UI widget at +0x3c
server_implication: The blank pre-existing-occupant row is likely sensitive to slot-table population and queued type-4 row ordering

observed_behavior: Builds the 0x22EC slot table, assigns slot states 1/2/3, then resolves room-user data back into row-facing state
server_implication: The client needs slot-table population before row-materialization can complete cleanly

observed_behavior: Switches the room UI widget at +0x3c between two states and mirrors that state to the child presenter at +0x54
server_implication: Room lock/open and related room-state changes share the same presenter family as roster/start UI

observed_behavior: Thin wrapper that forwards a boolean into the room UI presenter at +0x3c
server_implication: The roster/start screen has multiple small boolean-style presenter methods that may need exact event ordering

observed_behavior: Thin wrapper that resets or clears the room UI presenter at +0x3c
server_implication: This is part of the same presenter family used during room-state redraw

observed_behavior: Thin wrapper that forwards a second boolean-style state into the room UI presenter at +0x3c
server_implication: The active room screen uses multiple distinct per-slot or per-room booleans, not one flat state bit

observed_behavior: Thin wrapper that clears another room UI presenter state at +0x3c
server_implication: Presenter reset paths likely matter when rows are re-materialized during join

observed_behavior: Thin wrapper that clears the room UI presenter at +0x3c

observed_behavior: Sets a labeled mode/text field on the room UI presenter at +0x3c using param2/param3 selections

observed_behavior: Forwards a boolean into the secondary presenter object at +0x58
server_implication: A second presenter object participates in the same active room UI contract

observed_behavior: Applies lobby attribute changes by object id and tag, then emits completion code
server_implication: Lobby attribute change callbacks are split from generic lobby query handling and have their own success/failure semantics

observed_behavior: Handles changed user-property data and forwards payload to a dedicated helper
server_implication: User property changes are separate from lobby name/user-count updates and should not be flattened together

observed_behavior: Reads room-user payload fields at +0x10/+0x14/+0x18, prepares a name buffer, and logs UserID:%d NAME:%s
server_implication: Room-user payload layout matters beyond the first query callback and feeds later room lifecycle updates too

observed_behavior: Branches on GAME/NAME/USER tags and only triggers extra refresh behavior for some tags
server_implication: Client refresh behavior depends on tag identity, so Kage should preserve which attribute changed

observed_behavior: Small shared callback shim in the same room-user/lobby-follow-up family; pass22 range recovery did not reveal a clean standalone leave handler beside it
server_implication: The leave/update path likely shares tagged callback machinery instead of living in one isolated "user left" function

observed_behavior: Aligned/unaligned string-copy helper, not a room callback
server_implication: Avoid misclassifying helper routines as protocol handlers when tracing room-user flow

observed_behavior: Shows A game was not able to be started. and sets a local state field to 8
server_implication: Bomberman has an explicit failed-start state that Kage must avoid tripping

observed_behavior: Builds a compact 4-byte pre-match packet, chooses opcode 0x0b or 0x0c, derives a boolean from counter/state comparisons, and sends it
server_implication: Pre-match rule/start progression likely depends on a compact ready/start handshake, not only room attributes

observed_behavior: Builds a subtype 9 packet with a 9-byte payload from local pre-match state
server_implication: Bomberman has a real pre-match subtype 9 path; older Kage assumptions that subtype 9 is unused are unsafe

observed_behavior: When counters and states line up, tears down several UI objects, clears the current pre-match object, and advances local state to 0x0b
server_implication: The client has a concrete post-handshake transition gate before match start, so Kage must satisfy the same readiness conditions

observed_behavior: Allocates or initializes a 0x4c-byte object and zeros key fields
server_implication: Room-user related data feeds a structured object model, not a loose list of strings

observed_behavior: Resets that object and optionally frees it depending on flags
server_implication: Leave/update flows likely involve explicit object reset semantics in addition to payload application

observed_behavior: Reads a byte tag at offset +0x12, indexes a table of offsets and handlers, and dispatches to a tag-specific worker
server_implication: Room-user and related payloads are probably type-dispatched, which raises the cost of sending semantically mixed records

observed_behavior: Builds a subtype 4 packet with a boolean and 32-bit parameter from local pre-match state
server_implication: Bomberman has a real pre-match subtype 4 path that should not be treated as dead code

observed_behavior: Confirmed UDP 0x0a roster/slot-table update builder; fills the +0x22ec slot table, resolves users into the +0x238c visible-name table, and feeds the 0x8C096928 room UI gate
server_implication: Keep udpA total-slot shape stable; use it to materialize slot/name rows, but do not conflate it with ping occupied-slot liveness

observed_behavior: Final owner-side Start Battle enable gate requires +0x00e0, locked room bit 0x40000000, rendered-name count > 1, and rendered-name count equal to occupied bits from +0x00e4
server_implication: Kage now replies to Bomberman ping with aggregate occupied slot mask (03 for two one-slot clients) instead of echoing each sender's local 01/02 mask

observed_behavior: Receives NewKeyHolderInfo, stores owner id at +0x2448 and owner position at +0x244c, then calls 0x8C0F4E64(true) only when the received owner id matches local self
server_implication: Kage now sends Bomberman udp 0x0e keyholder sync after room-init ACK when all human room occupants are joined, instead of only on owner changes

observed_behavior: Toggles bit 0x4 at room UI offset +0x00a8 according to the local keyholder/owner boolean
server_implication: Owner-side UI may keep Start Battle disabled unless the owner client has consumed a keyholder sync in the active room screen

observed_behavior: Incoming server-side Bomberman command 0x0b copies 9 bytes from payload+4 into +0x245c and sets +0x00e0=1
server_implication: Kage must send current rules as server-to-client udp 0x0b command+word+9 rule bytes; do not relay compact client 0x0b as-is

observed_behavior: Incoming server-side command 0x0c parses a larger structured count/map payload from payload+4, not a 4-byte compact ready packet
server_implication: Kage should not relay compact client 0x0c as-is until the structured server payload is fully decoded

observed_behavior: Incoming server-side Bomberman command 0x11 copies a 32-bit value from payload+4 into client state +0x00e4
server_implication: The final Start Battle gate counts occupied bits from +0x00e4, so Kage now broadcasts udp 0x11 command+word+mask after join/rule sync; expected two-human one-slot mask is 03

observed_behavior: Incoming server-side Bomberman command 0x13 tears down the room UI, sets room/game state 0x0b, calls 0x8C09A3BC to build play-game setup data, and stores payload+4 as a start token
server_implication: Kage must not relay client-origin start command 0x0a as server 0x0a; server 0x0a is the roster/slot-table builder. Kage now broadcasts server 0x13 after validated Start Battle.

observed_behavior: After server 0x13, both Dreamcasts reached the game board and emitted client REQ_GAME_DATA commands 0x0d, 0x05, 0x1a, 0x1b, and 0x0e; 0x1a/0x1b carry 128-byte map-info blocks
server_implication: Kage now ACKs and relays these exact payloads as REQ_GAME_DATA with source player id preserved; if the board still freezes after these relays, investigate server command 0x14 Game Time Info next

observed_behavior: After deferred post-map command 0x14, the board loaded with two player sprites and both clients emitted repeated REQ_GAME_DATA commands 0x01/full=02c8/size=200, 0x02/full=04a4/size=164, and 0x03/full=063c/size=60; Kage previously logged them as unhandled immediately before the disconnect popup/timed out state
server_implication: Kage now relays these exact live-state payloads to the other room players using the same REQ_GAME_DATA relay path as map/state packets; no room/rule/start/map timing shape changed

observed_behavior: Server-side Bomberman command 0x14 dispatches through 0x8C093B70 into 0x8C099E92, which reads payload+4/start frame, payload+8/end frame, and payload+0x0c/common frame, then logs Game Time Info / Start_Frame / End_Frame
server_implication: Fresh 21_06-49-50 live evidence proved clients ACK immediate 0x14 after 0x13 but then stop before 0x1a/0x1b map blocks, so Kage now defers 0x14 until all real players send post-map REQ_GAME_DATA 0x0e; current frame payload remains start=0 end=10800 common=0 for validation

observed_behavior: Server-side command 0x16 reads a 32-bit value from payload+4, logs Svr Settled Recv Dead Bits, stores it into play/game state, and refreshes the state machine
server_implication: This is a later settle/dead-bit path; keep it for end-of-battle mapping, not the immediate board-population fix

observed_behavior: Server-side command 0x19 walks the 8-slot table, reads a 32-bit value from payload+4 when state allows, logs Svr Completed ed Recv Dead Bits, stores it, and refreshes the state machine
server_implication: This is a later completed/dead-bit path for win/lose/settle; implement only once live logs reach battle settlement

observed_behavior: Server-side command 0x1c reads value from payload+4 and mask from payload+8, then writes the low value byte into global+0x270+slot*8 and clears the per-slot timer/counter at +4
server_implication: Fresh 21_08-34-52 logs showed 0x01/0x02/0x03 live-state relays worked but no outbound post-start 0x1c existed before delayed disconnect; Kage now sends in-game 0x1c liveness sync after post-map 0x14 and every second while InGame

observed_behavior: Server-side command 0x01 copies/decodes live-state records and marks the 0x01 buffer as refreshed
server_implication: Fresh 21_10-04-34 proved sender-echo regressed live play; Kage keeps peer-only relay for 0x01 while the true bomb/input authority path is mapped

observed_behavior: Server-side command 0x02 parses player records plus 28 compact 4-byte object records through 0x8C0DD698
server_implication: Temporary yellow A-press mark with no bomb commit plus constant 0x02 object records proves the client is not sending a committed bomb object; sender-echo regressed and was removed

observed_behavior: Decodes a 4-byte compact object/live record into several bitfields
server_implication: Use this helper when decoding bomb/object deltas around A-press tests

observed_behavior: Server-side command 0x03 parses a compact 8-slot live-state buffer
server_implication: Fresh 21_10-04-34 proved 0x03 sender-echo regressed live play; keep peer-only relay with source player id preserved

observed_behavior: Server-side command 0x15 clears/advances battle state and can move the play state toward end cleanup
server_implication: Fresh 21_09-17-33 had no post-timer 0x15; Kage now sends 0x16/0x19/0x15 after the 10800-frame match timer expires

observed_behavior: Local battle state machine reacts to refreshed state values including 0x13, 0x15, and 0x1a
server_implication: Use this when validating whether server 0x16/0x19/0x15 leads to results/return-to-room or exposes another missing packet
